text.title: Work Breakdown Structure Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules — every WBS must satisfy all five.

rule.statement: The WBS at every level MUST sum to exactly 100% of the parent scope — no overlap, no gap.
rule.rationale: Overlap creates double-counting; gaps cause silent feature loss.

rule.statement: WBS elements MUST be noun-led deliverables; verb-led activities belong to schedule-development.
Leaf work packages MUST be estimable in 8-80 hours and assignable to a single named owner.
rule.rationale: Above 80h, packages hide estimation risk; below 8h, the WBS becomes a task list.

rule.statement: Every work package MUST have a dictionary entry with owner, estimate, inputs, outputs, acceptance criteria.
rule.rationale: Outline alone is ambiguous; dictionary makes acceptance verifiable.

rule.statement: Packages outside the signed scope baseline are forbidden; gold-plated entries must be rejected.
rule.rationale: Gold-plating consumes capacity and breaks fixed-bid margins.
